notes: Stock-Bitmain SHA-256 manifest. Daemon preflight matches the staged tarball SHA against this list and emits a SafetyFinding at one of four severities:
platform_signature is a coarse fingerprint matched against /proc/cpuinfo + future board-detect output.

Wave 12 W12-B added 4 new entries for the office fleet (S19j Pro BB / S19k Pro / S21 / S19j Pro Amlogic). These mirror PROFILE_TABLE entries in `dcentrald-api/src/routes/restore_to_stock.rs` — `dcentos_revertable: "live-test-required"` until W13 runs the full preflight + NAND backup + flash + reboot loop on real hardware.

Entries with sha256:`UNKNOWN` are placeholder rows for models we do not have a confirmed stock image for in the local archive — they let the operator see what the daemon WOULD recognize once a hash lands. The lookup helper treats UNKNOWN entries as not-found.
DO NOT INVENT SHAs. If you need to add a new entry, hash a real on-disk file.

stock_images.revert_notes: Pre-July-2019 stock S9 firmware (550MHz autofreq). UNSIGNED — slot-flip via `fw_setenv firmware <inactive>` works clean and DCENT_OS can be flashed back over it without signing-oracle dependency. Recommended default for S9 home-test revert. + .

Post-July-2019 SIGNED stock S9 firmware. Bitmain shipped the signature lock in this build — once installed, the bootloader refuses unsigned DCENT_OS images on update. Operator must factory-bootloader-flash via SD card or downgrade to a pre-July-2019 stock first..

No confirmed Bitmain factory stock S17 image present in the local archive. The vnish-farm ‘original’ tarballs are VNish’s preserved copies and may have been touched. Treat S17 stock revert as MANUAL (SD-card recovery) until a verified stock image is hashed and added.

Stock Bitmain S19 Pro signed merge-release. Has Bitmain signature gate AND the am2 SecureBoot path; we do NOT have proof that DCENT_OS can be re-flashed over this without going through SD-card recovery. Treat as `dcentos_revertable: false` until live-tested. + brick-back notes for am2.

Stock Bitmain S19j Pro Zynq am2 merge-release. Same signature + secure-boot caveats as S19 Pro stock. Additionally: am2 kernel ships without /dev/gpiochip and our libgpiod paths are no-op on .139, so revert leaves the operator on a stock that still needs careful re-deploy. Do NOT auto-flash..

Amlogic A113D-based S19j Pro variant. STRICT no-revert default:  documents that vnish.farm unlock packs include an Amlogic_S21/SECURE_BOOT_SET (1024B, SHA256 c3b77476bfc640ed…) blob that flips the A113D SECURE_BOOT eFuse irreversibly — eFuse damage is unrecoverable. Stock images themselves are clean, but our preflight must keep this model on `dcentos_revertable:false` until an in-process IOC scan proves the staged tarball is BSB-free.
Sister build to S19j Pro but different ASIC count. Same Zynq am2 caveats — revert is MANUAL until live-tested.

Stock Bitmain S19 base merge-release. Same Zynq am2 caveats. Treat as not-revertable until proven. size_bytes filled 2026-05-29 (cont.21, gap-swarm G31) from the on-disk archive, sha256-verified against the recorded hash.

S21 Amlogic A113D NoPic. Same SECURE_BOOT eFuse hazard as S19j Pro Amlogic —. Default to dcentos_revertable:false; only flip to true after IOC scan proves no SECURE_BOOT_SET blob and live-test passes.
Stock Bitmain S21 Pro Amlogic AML release. Same SECURE_BOOT caveats as S21. Hash is real (file present), but revertability is gated on wave-11 platform expansion.

S19k Pro Amlogic A113D + BM1366 + BHB56902 hashboards (0x05 0x11 EEPROM preamble) + APW121215f PSU fw=0x76.. Stock revert path is SD-card recovery only — Buildroot fw_setenv path is not validated for am3-aml in production.

Wave 12 W12-B PROFILE_TABLE.am335x-bb-bm1362 entry. AM335x BeagleBone BB stock-Bitmain S19j Pro variant (TI Sitara, NOT Zynq, NOT Amlogic). Code path complete via `revert_to_stock_am335x_bb.sh`; PROFILE_TABLE.verified_revertable=false until live test on the office BB unit. Stock SSH default `miner:miner` (root has /bin/false). mtd partition list is conservative-default-with-honest-comment; W13 must validate against `cat /proc/mtd` on real hardware. uart_trans.ko is the ASIC UART shim — not load-bearing for the flash itself.

Wave 12 W12-B PROFILE_TABLE.amlogic-am3-bm1366 entry. Code path complete via `revert_to_stock_am3_aml_s19k.sh` (uImage at /dev/mtd5 offset 0x5100000). PROFILE_TABLE.verified_revertable=false until live test on the office S19k Pro. Same SHA as the wave-10 entry above; the W12 row is the explicit pin for the W13 live-test loop.

Wave 12 W12-B PROFILE_TABLE.amlogic-am3-bm1368 entry. Code path complete via `revert_to_stock_am3_aml_s21.sh` (uImage at /dev/mtd5 offset 0x5100000). PROFILE_TABLE.verified_revertable=false until live test on the office S21. SHA is computed from the local vnish-farm capture (.zip — operator must extract a clean .bmu before staging). SECURE_BOOT_SET hazard is enforced by the wave-≤11 IOC scanner regardless of this entry.

Wave 12 W12-B placeholder for the PROFILE_TABLE.amlogic-am3-bm1362 row. The S19j Pro Amlogic A113D variant uses the same uImage flash mechanism as the S21 (mtd5 + offset 0x5100000); a dedicated revert script can reuse `revert_to_stock_am3_aml_s21.sh` until BM1362-specific differences emerge from live testing. SHA is the S19j Pro-A merge-release blob — closest stock match in the local archive; the operator should swap to the exact-target build (S19j Pro non-A) once one is hashed.
